items.title: U.A.E. reports drone strike at nuclear power plant as Iran war deadlock endures
items.summary_auto: A drone strike caused a fire at a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, officials in Abu Dhabi said on Sunday, at a time when progress appears to have stalled in efforts to end the U.S.-Israeli  war with Iran  and restart shipping in the Gulf.

items.title: Canadian, U.S. fascist fight clubs joining forces south of the border, CBC investigation finds
This blind Yukon birder learned to use his ears — now hearing loss means every spring is quieter
items.summary_auto: 76-year-old Brian Lendrum, who is blind, spent decades learning to locate and identify birds by sound. But now, he says, as he ages his hearing is “just very, very gradually going down every year.”

items.title: What about endangered turtles? As Ontario fast-tracks transmission line, environmental questions emerge
items.summary_auto: Ontario's plans to fast-track a major new transmission line between Barrie and Sudbury is raising eyebrows about the possible impact on the environment. Advocates are questioning how the project — a 500-kilovolt line by Hydro One — could affect wetlands, lakes, wildlife habitat and species at risk.

items.title: Princess of Wales steps out on world stage — and sends signals for Royal Family — in solo sojourn to Italy
items.summary_auto: A two-day solo trip to Italy by the Princess of Wales sent signals both for Catherine herself and her role within the Royal Family on the world stage.

items.title: Voice notes on vinyl records? It used to be all the rage
items.summary_auto: Thomas Levin has amassed a large collection of "voice letters" from the 1930s through to the 1960s. Using public booths, people could record messages on to a vinyl record, and dispatch them in the mail.

items.title: Moving to Canada was an unpleasant shock. Then I experienced my first snowfall
items.summary_auto: Moving to Canada is a dream for many in Dubai, writes Sidra Mundia, but the challenges of living in Regina came as a rude shock. Her first snowfall brought a reset.

items.title: Court won't hear case against Kingston doctor ordered to pay back $600k for COVID vaccines
items.summary_auto: Dr. Elaine Ma, the Kingston, Ont., physician who organized dozens of vaccine clinics early in the COVID-19 pandemic — and who was ordered to pay back hundreds of thousands of dollars in fees for those services — says she's feeling somewhat relieved after the latest turn in her legal ordeal.

items.title: Marketing your bar for the World Cup? FIFA’s strict trademark rules could land you a cease and desist
items.summary_auto: To get a sense of what the rules around FIFA branding are, CBC News went through its publicly shared intellectual property guidelines and its hosting addendum with the city.

items.title: From LeBron to DeRozan: Breaking down the sports references on Drake's new triple album
items.summary_auto: No musical artist, past or present, has immersed themselves in the culture and subeconomy of sport more than Canada’s very own Aubrey “Drake” Graham — and the infatuation is mutual.

items.title: FIFA is notorious for strict copyright rules. Here’s how Toronto businesses are navigating them
items.summary_auto: To get a sense of what the rules around FIFA branding are, CBC News went through its publicly shared intellectual property guidelines and its hosting addendum with the city.

items.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Congo, Uganda an emergency of international concern
Sabres quiet Bell Centre crowd with 7 unanswered, beat Habs to force Game 7
items.summary_auto: The Buffalo Sabres roared back from a two-goal deficit with seven unanswered goals to defeat the Montreal Canadiens 8-3 at the Bell Centre on Saturday, forcing a Game 7. The winner on Monday will face the Carolina Hurricanes in the Eastern Conference final.

items.title: B.C. approved logging in threatened caribou habitat despite provincial recommendation against it
items.summary_auto: B.C. 's Ministry of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship warned that proposed logging near Quesnel Lake would harm threatened southern mountain caribou habitat. But the Ministry of Forests approved logging in the area anyway, drawing criticism from residents and conservation groups.

items.title: Bulgaria wins its 1st Eurovision final over Israel in politically clouded song contest
items.summary_auto: Bulgaria has won the 70th Eurovision song contest with singer Dara's party anthem Bangaranga, beating 24 other competitors during Saturday's grand final in Vienna. Israeli's Noam Bettan came in second after the contest faced protests against the country's participation.

items.title: Montreal Victoire move closer to Walter Cup title with Game 2 overtime win over Ottawa Charge
items.summary_auto:
Maggie Flaherty scored 14:12 into overtime to give
the Montreal Victoire a 2-1 win over the Ottawa Charge in Game 2 of
the PWHL's Walter Cup final on Saturday at Place Bell in Laval, Que.

items.title: Yukoner isolating in B.C. tests presumptively positive for hantavirus
items.summary_auto: One of two Yukoners who have been isolating in British Columbia after hantavirus broke out on their cruise ship has now presumptively tested positive for the virus. The couple, who are in their 70s, were required to isolate for a minimum of 21 days after vacationing on the MV Hondius.

items.title: Driver plows into pedestrians in Italy's Modena, leaving 8 injured
items.summary_auto: A driver plowed into pedestrians in the northern Italian city of Modena on Saturday, injuring eight people, four of them critically, local authorities said.
